concept: Split / Divide in Two / Part Along the Grain
description: To split: firewood along its grain, the seed case at sprouting, the path where it forks. Division into parts — where 'kati' (cut) uses an edge, 'pisha' follows the thing's own line of parting.
sound_symbolism: Fine 'pi' finds the seam, 'sha' shears it open — the sound of clean parting.
grammatical_notes: Transitive: 'mia wolea pisha.' (I split the wood.) Naturally: 'thinoe pisha.' (The seed splits.) The path: 'ruela pisha.' (The path forks.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: What parts along its own seam parts without violence; the verb distinguishes natural division from forced breaking.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Wood splits true along its grain and ruins the blade against it — splitting is the craft of working with a thing's nature, not against it.
